---
title: "About"
---

::::: about-container
::: about-image
<img src="images/profile_rijkslib.jpg" alt="Fuminari Miura"/>
:::

::: about-text
I am a Senior Researcher at the Dutch National Institute for Public Health and the Environment [(RIVM)](https://www.rivm.nl/f-fuminari-miura-phd), jointly appointed with Nagasaki University, Japan.

My work focuses on understanding how transmission processes unfold across heterogeneous populations and how epidemic theory can inform practical and timely control strategies.

<!--Mission
My research combines mathematical modelling, statistical methods, and empirical data to characterise transmission dynamics. The aim is to support adaptive and equitable decision-making in infectious disease control.
-->

<!--Vision
A future where epidemic response is guided not only by prediction, but by structure-aware, decision-relevant modelling under uncertainty.
-->

:::
:::::
